course_descriptor: LazyEarn track, School of Hard Knocks millionaire interview series
dynamic_book_descriptor: A question-organized dynamic field book from the full 10 Questions with a Millionaire playlist
dynamic_book_target: build a real dynamic book from all 22 10 Questions with a Millionaire transcripts; do not make one chapter per video. Treat the repeated interview format as a union of questions and answers across the whole corpus. Each lecture should feed evidence into a growing set of roughly ten or more durable questions about wealth, business, risk, ownership, skill, scale, taxes, relationships, failure, advice, and what money is for.
dynamic_book_style_target: make the book intriguing, story-rich, and source-conscious. Preserve the interview energy, named cases, numbers, reversals, and practical mechanisms, but organize them into a coherent reader-facing book rather than a transcript digest. Use vivid narrative openings, compact mechanisms, Q&A blocks, tables, and occasional transcript-grounded diagrams when useful.
dynamic_book_structure_target: start from this rough question order and adjust conservatively as the 22 lectures accumulate: 1. What was the first real money-making mechanism? 2. What painful bottleneck or customer problem created the opportunity? 3. What did the person actually own or control? 4. How did customers, distribution, reputation, or attention arrive? 5. How was risk financed, survived, or transferred? 6. Which skill or operating discipline compounded fastest? 7. How did income become enterprise value, equity, or durable wealth? 8. What did taxes, leverage, legal structure, or financial engineering change? 9. What failure, loss, or humiliation taught the rule? 10. Which relationships, networks, trust, or teams mattered? 11. What advice would survive for a beginner or younger self? 12. What is wealth for after the number is reached? Keep these questions as the book spine; distribute each new lecture's evidence into the best-fitting question chapter rather than preserving video order. Repetition is acceptable when a new interview gives a new named witness, number, contradiction, or sharper story for an existing question.
deliverable_target: a faithful, source-conscious chapter for 10 Questions with a Millionaire, plus a per-course compiled PDF book.
visual_target: selected frames are supporting evidence for names, captions, and interview context; do not let them override the spoken content.
mathematical_standard: preserve concrete claims, business details, and any quantitative or institutional statements, but avoid invented textbook exposition.
style_target: notes should read like a careful interview-derived companion text for wealth, entrepreneurship, and commercial judgment, with explicit separation between anecdote, claim, and mechanism.
credit_target: keep explicit credit to School of Hard Knocks and curation by LazyingArt LLC; reserve the website URL for front matter, not body prose.
front_matter_single: Original interview by School of Hard Knocks. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
front_matter_plural: Original interviews by School of Hard Knocks. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
reference_missing_text: No directly matching reference PDF is available for this course.
reference_no_match_text: Matching course reference PDFs were found, but no strong topical excerpt was automatically matched for this interview.
reference_budget_text: Matching course reference PDFs were found, but no excerpt fit within the current prompt budget.
